scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: Identifies Lobsters as a Rails social news site, lists the standard Rails directories including app/mailboxes, pins Story at app/models/story.rb:3-1386, and names ApplicationRecord as the base — enough for targeted lookups. However, lib/extras (markdowner, etc.) is mentioned only as 'lib/' without calling out the extras subdirectory, and the shared behavior beyond ApplicationRecord (the Token concern) is entirely absent from this orientation step.

specificity.rationale: Four paths with line ranges are given (story.rb:3-1386, application_record.rb:3-8, comment.rb:3-633, vote.rb:3-198), which is concrete. The Token concern (app/models/concerns/token.rb), any helper, and lib/extras are absent, so shared-piece coverage is thin.

justification.rationale: The answer labels Story as 'user-submitted content with title, URL/description, tags, comments, votes' but does not explain WHY it is the architectural center — i.e., that all other records (comments, votes, taggings, links) hang off it, making it the hub the rest of the app references. The why is treated as self-evident.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; the answer presents every path and claim as confirmed, even though the tool-call preamble is vague and lib/extras is unverified.

relationship.rationale: The summary says Story has 'tags, comments, votes' but states no connecting relationship (no has_many, no belongs_to, no mention of the Token concern supplying token-based IDs); the four files are listed without any edge between them, so the agent learns endpoints but not the graph.

steps.step: Map the Story contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps the Token concern at app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19, lists all major associations (belongs_to user/domain/origin/merged_into_story, has_many taggings/comments/votes/hidings/savings/links), and identifies constants like FLAGGABLE_DAYS and HOTNESS_WINDOW — enough for an agent to proceed with targeted follow-ups. However, specific line numbers within story.rb for individual contract declarations are absent (the whole file is cited as 3-1386), so the agent must open the file to locate each piece.

specificity.rationale: The Token concern gets exact file:line (app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19), constants are named, and association targets are spelled out (Vote, Comment, Tagging, HiddenStory, SavedStory, Link). No per-association line numbers within story.rb are given, and scopes like `base`, `hottest`, and `not_deleted` are named but lack line citations.

justification.rationale: The answer describes WHAT each piece does ('auto-generates TypeID tokens', 'touch: true touches story') but does not explain WHY the contract is shaped this way — for instance, why token-based IDs are used, or why so many records hang off Story as the central submission object rather than through an intermediate model.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere: the answer presents file names, line numbers, and contract pieces with uniform confidence, without noting any parts it could not confirm from the index or that require manual verification.

relationship.rationale: The answer states the relation type for each binding — belongs_to user/domain/origin, has_many taggings/comments/votes — and characterises the Token concern by what it supplies. It does not state dependent options (destroy vs restrict) or call out the `through:` / `source:` path for derived associations, leaving the agent to verify those connecting details itself.

steps.step: Trace how a story is created and ranked

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the controller entry (stories_controller.rb:21), assign_initial_attributes (story.rb:543), and the after_create/after_save callback sequence with file:line, giving the agent a usable skeleton. However, the markdown rendering path is described as going through a cron job and references comment.rb:334 rather than tracing the actual story-creation rendering path, and the URL normalization chain (Story#url= → set_domain_and_origin) is mentioned but not woven into the creation sequence order.

specificity.rationale: Most layers carry concrete file:line (stories_controller.rb:21, story.rb:543/846/867/1132/1136/1157/1168, story.rb:550-580); the markdown cite (comment.rb:334) is a wrong-file reference for the story path, and story_text.rb:3 is named but the cron mechanism is left abstract.

justification.rationale: The ranking formula gets a brief 'why' (log(score) + comment points + tag hotness modifiers), but the callbacks — mark_submitter, record_initial_upvote, recreate_links — are listed without explaining why each must fire at creation (e.g., why an initial upvote is created for the submitter, or why links are rebuilt on save).

uncertainty.rationale: The answer notes story_text is produced by a cron job rather than inline, which is a useful flag, but it does not call out which steps it could not open fully (e.g., whether bust_comment_redirect_cache or update_story_text lines were verified vs. inferred).

relationship.rationale: The creation_order list and the arrow-notation summary do assemble a controller → callbacks chain, but the URL handling (Story#url= → set_domain_and_origin) and markdown rendering are presented as separate prose blocks rather than spliced into the one explicit chain; the agent cannot directly read off the full submission → URL → markdown → score path without stitching the sections together.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Story contract

map_quality.rationale: All six rubric areas are covered with named dependents at file:line (InboxMailbox#parent at inbox_mailbox.rb:34, Stats.fill_stories_graph_cache at stats.rb:30, Mod::FlaggedController at mod/flagged_controller.rb:9, etc.), and the model_compositions block enumerates every belongs_to/has_many pair. The markdown re-renderer (Markdowner) is mentioned only in the step_3 creation chain and absent from step_4's grouped dependents, and the HomeController search entries are collapsed to 'all Story scopes' rather than listed individually.

specificity.rationale: Most entries carry file:line and a one-phrase description ("Story.where(short_id: ...) finds parent story for email replies", "Story.pluck(:created_at)"), but ModNote.tattle_on_story_domain!/origin!/brigading omits line numbers, HomeController entries are batched rather than individually listed, and the Markdowner dependent is entirely absent from the grouped output.

justification.rationale: Grouping by area (content_rendering, search, statistics, voting_flagging, inbound_email, moderation) implicitly signals why each cluster is affected, and the risk_ranking block in step_6 explains per-dependent risk reasons. However, within step_4's grouped lists the per-dependent WHY is thin — the answer names what each dependent does with the story but rarely explains why that makes it a regression risk.

uncertainty.rationale: Step 4 itself contains no acknowledgment that a static pass may miss concern-reached or derive-only dependents; the caveat about 'indirect, concern- or derive-reached' appears only in step_6's blast-radius section, so an agent reading step_4 alone would not know to verify indirect paths.

relationship.rationale: The model_compositions block explicitly states the association type for every satellite model ("Vote belongs_to story: vote.rb:5", "Comment belongs_to story: comment.rb:5 with touch: true"), and action-dependent entries state the call path ("Story.where(short_id: ...)", "Story.base used in query", "story.update_score_and_recalculate!"). The Markdowner — the rubric's named non-obvious content dependent — has no connecting-relationship entry in step_4, and concern-based or polymorphic paths are not made explicit.

steps.step: Trace how story identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer lists guard methods in story.rb with file:line (is_gone? :761-763, can_be_seen_by_user? :582-584, not_deleted scope :76) and notes CommentsController :17-20, but never traces guard usage inside the four areas the prompt names — search indexing, stats, inbound-email, moderation — leaving the agent to open each of those files to find whether guards are applied or absent.

specificity.rationale: Story model guard methods are cited with precise line ranges, but the answer gives no file:line for where search.rb, stats.rb, inbox_mailbox.rb, or the mod controllers invoke (or skip) those guards; the agent still needs targeted reads in each area.

justification.rationale: is_gone? is explained ('is_deleted? or banned user with negative score') but the answer does not explain WHY each downstream path — a search indexer touching a deleted story, a stats pluck including a hidden one, a mailbox routing to a gone story — must re-confirm visibility before acting.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no gaps; search (Story.base scope) and stats (Story.pluck with no guard noted) likely have implicit or absent visibility checks that should have been flagged as unconfirmed rather than silently omitted.

relationship.rationale: Guard methods are catalogued in isolation from story.rb; the answer does not state the path by which, e.g., InboxMailbox#parent reaches is_gone? (or does not), or how Search#perform_story_search! relies on the 'base' scope as its visibility gate — methods are named but not connected to the dependents they protect.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers all required areas — rendering, search, stats, voting/flagging, inbound email, moderation — with file:line references and a ranked CRITICAL/HIGH list of 11 items; aggregate figures ('75 direct callers, 258 total affected symbols across 46 files') give the agent useful scale. Minor confusion in a few attributions (Vote#target cited as 'story.rb:185' instead of 'vote.rb:185') slightly blunts usability but does not require a fresh exploration.

specificity.rationale: Most at-risk dependents are named with exact file:line and a reason: 'HiddenStory.hide_story_for_user hidden_story.rb:13 — calls story.update_score_and_recalculate!', 'InboxMailbox#parent story.rb:45 — Story.where(short_id: ...)', etc. The Vote#target file misattribution ('story.rb:185' vs 'vote.rb:185') and a few vague entries ('app/controllers/home_controller.rb all Story scopes') prevent a higher score.

justification.rationale: Each ranked item states WHY it is risky: 'will break if story ID strategy changes', 'restrict_with_exception — cannot delete story if story_text exists', 'counter_cache stories_count cached column', 'touch:true modifies story's updated_at'. The causal chain is clear enough for the agent to triage without re-reading source.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer nowhere flags what it is less sure about — no mention of dependents that are concern- or association-reached and thus might be incomplete, no acknowledgment that a static pass could miss dynamic callers. 'Must_reverify' is a checklist, not an uncertainty signal.

relationship.rationale: The risk ranking explicitly names the connecting relationship for most dependents: 'belongs_to with touch:true', 'restrict_with_exception', 'calls update_score_and_recalculate!', 'Story.where(short_id: ...) lookup for email replies', 'counter_cache on belongs_to'. The few vague entries ('Search#perform_story_search! — uses Story.base scope') omit the join path, but the majority of the high-risk items carry their relationship.

map_quality.rationale: The map covers all six areas (rendering, search, stats, voting/flagging, email, moderation), names the Token concern and ApplicationRecord as shared pieces, and lists 9 spec files — broad structural coverage. However 'app/controllers/home_controller.rb all Story scopes' is explicitly vague, no insertion points within story.rb are given, extras/markdowner.rb from step 3 is absent from the map, and verification_steps are behavioral checklists rather than file:line edit targets a teammate could follow directly.

specificity.rationale: Many dependents carry concrete file:line (app/models/vote.rb:185, app/mailboxes/inbox_mailbox.rb:34, app/models/hidden_story.rb:13), but the spec files lack any line numbers or test-method names, the home controller entry is admitted vague, and the model_to_edit spans lines 3-1386 (the full file) rather than pinpointing what to change.

justification.rationale: The risk_ranking in step 6 contains the per-dependent rationale (e.g. 'touch:true modifies story updated_at', 'restrict_with_exception — cannot delete story if story_text exists'), but that context is not carried into the step_7_change_map section itself; the dependents_by_area is a bare file list with no inline 'why,' requiring a teammate to cross-reference an earlier section.

uncertainty.rationale: The map does not flag any dependent it could not confirm, any association it inferred rather than verified, or any spec file it guessed at; an agent reading the map would have no signal about which entries are solid versus speculative.

relationship.rationale: The dependents_by_area in step_7_change_map is a plain file list — no connecting relationship is stated inline (e.g. 'comment.rb:5 via belongs_to with touch:true', 'inbox_mailbox.rb:34 via Story.where(short_id:...)'); the connecting paths from step 4 and step 6 are not threaded into the final map, so the chain from Story contract change to each reviewed file is absent.
